kernels.summary: The semantic target is PyTorch scaled_dot_product_attention, but the matching PyTorch source checkout is not one of the allowed source roots.

interface_definition.rationale: The KID interface is known, but its Python definition cannot be verified without the PyTorch source repository.

kernel_impl.rationale: The dispatcher and native SDPA implementation are expected to exist in PyTorch, but no allowed root contains them.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: The Python-to-native dispatcher path cannot be verified without PyTorch sources.

kernel_header.rationale: Potential native declarations cannot be checked because the owning repository is absent.

gaps: PyTorch source is absent from the manifest, so none of the four layers can be evidenced within allowed roots.
kernels.manual_followup: Add a matching PyTorch source checkout to the manifest and rerun locate and source_locate.

kernels.summary: The SGLang wrapper directly launches a Triton kernel in the same module; there is no native bridge or independent header.

interface_definition.rationale: The definition matches the KID call-site import and semantic target.

hits.reason: This is the imported Python wrapper used by the call site.

kernel_impl.rationale: The wrapper launches this Triton JIT kernel directly.

hits.reason: The @triton.jit decorator starts the executable Triton kernel definition that performs the target computation.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: The Python wrapper launches Triton directly and does not cross a Python/C++ binding.

kernel_header.rationale: The implementation is self-contained Triton code with no independent native declaration header.

kernels.summary: The Python torch.ops wrapper resolves through SGLang's Torch registration to a CUDA host launcher and FlashInfer device kernel.

interface_definition.rationale: The wrapper is the KID-selected public interface and calls torch.ops.sgl_kernel.silu_and_mul.

hits.reason: This Python definition is imported and invoked by the SGLang call site.

kernel_impl.rationale: The native launcher dispatches the templated FlashInfer device kernel in this order.

hits.reason: This is the registered CUDA host entry and launch site.
This templated CUDA kernel performs the elementwise activation and multiplication.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: Torch Library registration maps the Python torch.ops name to the native launcher.

hits.reason: The schema and CUDA implementation registration close the Python-to-native boundary.

kernel_header.rationale: The registered host function has a standalone public declaration.

hits.reason: This declaration connects the registration unit to the CUDA definition.

kernels.summary: The Python FlashAttention wrapper crosses SGLang's Torch registration into the sgl-attn host API, launch template, and SM90 device implementation.

interface_definition.rationale: This definition is the call-site-selected varlen FlashAttention interface.

hits.reason: The function is the Python interface invoked by SGLang attention code.

kernel_impl.rationale: The recorded order follows the native host entry, dispatch/launch template, then the SM90 kernel body.

hits.reason: This is the native host API reached by the registered operator.
This template specializes and launches the forward attention implementation.
This device operator is the core SM90 forward kernel implementation.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: The Torch Library schema/export anchors the Python operator to mha_fwd.

hits.reason: This registration exposes the forward native entry used by the Python wrapper.

kernel_header.rationale: The standalone header declares the native mha_fwd entry consumed by the extension.

hits.reason: This declaration connects the extension registration and sgl-attn definition.

kernels.summary: The Python wrapper builds a TVM-FFI JIT module from a CUDA header, invokes its host wrapper, and reaches the add_constant CUDA kernel.

interface_definition.rationale: This is the public JIT example function selected by KID.

hits.reason: The function allocates the result and invokes module.add_constant.

kernel_impl.rationale: The JIT-exported host wrapper launches the concrete CUDA kernel in this order.

hits.reason: The function template starts the TVM-FFI host function that validates inputs and launches the kernel.
The function template starts the CUDA global function that implements the scalar add.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: The load_jit call declares the CUDA source and generated wrapper exported as add_constant.

hits.reason: This loader configuration creates the Python-visible TVM-FFI module boundary.

kernel_header.rationale: The .cuh file contains executable host/device implementation rather than an independent declaration-only layer.

kernels.summary: The FlashInfer Python API obtains a generated sampling module, crosses its TVM-FFI binding, and reaches the CUDA launcher and rejection-sampling kernel.

interface_definition.rationale: This is the KID-selected fused top-k/top-p sampling API.

hits.reason: The public function performs the selected sampling operation.

kernel_impl.rationale: The native host entry calls the templated dispatcher, which launches the CUDA kernel.

hits.reason: This TVM-FFI host entry validates tensors and invokes the sampling implementation.
This template dispatches launch geometry and the deterministic specialization.
This CUDA kernel performs the fused rejection sampling loop.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: The generated module loader and its native declaration together establish the Python-to-CUDA boundary.

hits.reason: This builds and loads the Python-visible generated sampling module.
This binding declaration is used to export the native function into the generated module.

kernel_header.rationale: The .cuh file is implementation-bearing and is therefore part of kernel_impl, not a declaration-only header layer.

kernels.summary: The FlashInfer Python function launches a Triton RMSNorm kernel that uses a Triton scale-and-clamp helper; no native bridge or separate header exists.

interface_definition.rationale: The definition matches the KID interface and direct call-site import.

hits.reason: This wrapper computes launch parameters and invokes rms_norm_kernel.

kernel_impl.rationale: The main Triton kernel and its imported computation helper make up the implementation chain.

hits.reason: This Triton JIT function is the directly launched RMSNorm kernel.
This Triton helper implements the optional output scaling and clamping used by the kernel.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: The Python wrapper launches Triton without a C++ extension boundary.

kernel_header.rationale: The implementation is Python/Triton and has no independent native declaration header.

kernels.summary: The FlashInfer wrapper loads a generated module, invokes paged_run through TVM-FFI, and reaches the paged prefill host entry, dispatcher, and CUDA kernel.

This CUDA global function is the core paged prefill kernel.

py_cpp_binding.rationale: The Python generated-module loader and TVM-FFI paged_run export close the native boundary.

hits.reason: This creates the generated module whose paged_run function the wrapper calls.
This declaration is exported as paged_run by TVM-FFI.

kernel_header.rationale: The .cuh layer contains launch and device implementation, so it belongs to kernel_impl rather than a declaration-only header.

kernel_header.rationale: The CuTe DSL implementation is contained in Python and has no independent native declaration header.
